query: As of July 2026, has any U.S. Supreme Court justice announced retirement or resignation? Status of Alito, Thomas retirement speculation after NPR retracted its June 30 2026 story. Any current plans for a justice to retire in summer 2026.
answer:
I've completed my research. Here's the summary:

**As of July 8, 2026, no U.S. Supreme Court justice has announced a retirement or resignation.** The Court's nine-member roster remains intact.

**The NPR retraction (June 30, 2026):** On the final day of the Court's term, NPR erroneously published a story claiming Justice Alito was retiring. It was live for only ~6 minutes (10:51–10:57 a.m. ET) before being replaced with an editor's note, with an on-air correction at 11:07 a.m. The error occurred because veteran correspondent Nina Totenberg misheard a bystander saying "retirement announcements" (plural, referring to general/lower-court retirements) as a singular announcement about Alito — she did not personally hear any announcement from Chief Justice Roberts. NPR had a pre-written "obituary-style" story ready and published it without second confirmation. Totenberg called it "a rookie mistake," apologized to Alito personally, and NPR's Public Editor published a critical review.

**Alito & Thomas status:** Since April 2026, sources close to both justices have consistently told multiple outlets (Fox, ABC, CBS) that neither plans to retire in 2026. Alito (76) reportedly intends to serve into at least 2027 and has been actively hiring clerks for the 2026-2027 term. Thomas (77-78) also does not plan to step down.

**Context:** Speculation is driven by Republican hopes that Trump could replace the aging conservatives with younger appointees before the 2026 midterms, but reporting indicates no such plans exist as of July 2026.

The full structured findings have been submitted with 10 sources and 7 data points.

sources.title: NPR retracts story about Alito retirement
sources.snippet: NPR legal affairs correspondent Nina Totenberg misheard an announcement... Alito is not retiring. The story was wrong.

sources.title: Samuel Alito Is Not Retiring From Supreme Court—NPR Retracts Story Published In Error
sources.snippet: Alito has not announced his retirement as of Tuesday morning... sources told multiple outlets in April that neither justice was planning to retire in 2026.

sources.title: NPR retracts story reporting Justice Alito is retiring from Supreme Court
How NPR published a Supreme Court retirement that never happened
Supreme Court Justices Alito and Thomas not planning to retire this year, sources say
sources.snippet: Sources close to Alito confirm he is not planning to retire this year. Sources close to Thomas also say he does not plan to step down.

sources.title: Supreme Court Justices Alito, Thomas not expected to retire this year: Sources
sources.snippet: Alito, 76, has been hiring clerks for next term and intends to continue serving into at least 2027.

sources.title: Supreme Court Clerk Hiring Watch: Justice Alito's Non-Retirement
sources.snippet: Why would Justice Alito assemble a full complement of clerks... if he was planning on peacing out?

sources.title: Alito, Trump and Retirement Speculation at the Supreme Court
sources.snippet: Ed Whelan said Alito was now 'in the majority in a way that he couldn't count on in his first dozen years.'

sources.title: NPR retracts Alito retirement story within minutes
NPR Retracts Report That Justice Samuel Alito Is Retiring
sources.snippet: An official announcement that Justice Samuel Alito... sent the high court's press room scrambling.
